context: Go 1.24 CLI/TUI app in a single `ralph` module. Core code lives under `internal/app`, `internal/workflow`, `internal/shared`, `internal/tui`, and `internal/web`; the UI uses Bubble Tea, Bubbles, and Lip Gloss, and the web surface is a local HTTP/SSE server. Workflow execution starts in `internal/shared/session/session.go`, which calls `workflow.Driver.StartImplementation` in `internal/workflow/driver.go`. Workflow code is split into small phase files such as `internal/workflow/phase_generate.go` and `internal/workflow/phase_implement.go`, tests are mostly table-driven `*_test.go` files, PRDs are modeled in `internal/shared/prd/types.go`, and PRD persistence uses shared locking plus atomic saves in `internal/shared/prd/storage.go`. Git validation currently only checks that the workdir exists and contains a `.git` entry in `internal/shared/workdir/validate.go`. Primary test command: `go test ./...`; the repo also has `cd web && npm test` and `cd e2e && npx playwright test` for the browser surfaces.
test_spec: A temporary git repo checked out to a `feature/*` branch makes the new shared branch helper return that branch name and causes `workflow.Driver.StartImplementation` to skip the checkout path before any implementation slice runs. A temporary repo checked out to `main` still takes the checkout path before the first implementation slice prompt and uses the PRD branch name as the checkout target. Branch detection is table-driven and deterministic for `feature/*` versus non-feature branches, and the behavior is covered by Go tests in `internal/shared/workdir` and `internal/workflow`.

stories.title: Detect the active branch
stories.description: Add `CurrentBranchName(workDir string) (string, error)` and `IsFeatureBranch(branchName string) bool` in `internal/shared/workdir` so workflow code can read the active git branch without changing `ValidateGit`.

slices.behavior: The shared helper returns the current branch name from a real git worktree.
slices.red_hint: Write a failing test in `internal/shared/workdir` that creates a temporary git repo, runs `git checkout -b feature/test`, and asserts `CurrentBranchName(dir)` returns `feature/test`.
slices.refactor_hint: Reuse the same git command execution pattern already used by other shared git helpers.

slices.behavior: The shared helper classifies `feature/*` branches as feature branches and rejects non-feature branches.
slices.red_hint: Write failing table-driven tests for `IsFeatureBranch` that cover `feature/test`, `main`, and `release/1.0`.
slices.refactor_hint: Keep the branch-name check isolated so workflow code only depends on a small predicate.

stories.title: Skip redundant checkout
stories.description: Update `workflow.Driver.StartImplementation` and `session.Session.StartImplementationFromPRD` so implementation startup skips checkout when the active branch already matches `feature/*`, and otherwise checks out `p.BranchName` before the first implementation slice runs.

slices.behavior: Starting implementation on an existing feature branch does not invoke a checkout command.
slices.red_hint: Write a failing workflow test that uses a stubbed checkout helper, checks out `feature/test`, starts implementation from a PRD, and asserts the checkout helper is not called.

slices.behavior: Starting implementation on `main` checks out the PRD branch before the first implementation slice runs.
slices.red_hint: Write a failing workflow test that uses the same checkout spy, checks out `main`, starts implementation from a PRD with `branch_name` set to a feature branch, and asserts the checkout helper is called once with that branch name.
